Event: Lets out a big "NO!" when Will announces tough rival
Explanation: Announcement gives useful info but mainly demoralizes the club without clear benefit shown.

Event: Tina sheds shyness and gains confidence over time
Explanation: Her gradual growth benefits herself and helps the group by making her more capable.

Event: Tina becomes a very good dancer
Explanation: Improved skill helps her and strengthens performances for the team.

Event: Writers progressively lose interest in Tina
Explanation: They reallocate focus to more popular characters (benefit to them) while sidelining her.

Event: Sue summarizes Tina’s history and lampshades her stutter
Explanation: Meta-commentary acknowledges her development and gives her arc visibility with no shown harm.

Event: Tina initially takes interest in Mike because of his abs
Explanation: Mutual attraction leads to a later stable relationship; benefits both with no shown harm.

Event: Tina intensely stares at Blaine’s ass while he bends
Explanation: She indulges her crush while objectifying him slightly; mild gain for her, some discomfort implied.

Event: Series gives Tina an episode with an actual plot
Explanation: Giving her a limelight episode benefits her character and enriches the show.

Event: Tina develops into a deadpan snarker
Explanation: Sharper wit gives her voice and is mostly enjoyed by others, with only mild snarky edge.

Event: Blaine falls asleep with a cold while studying
Explanation: His illness and falling asleep harms him short-term while giving Tina proximity and concern opportunities.

Event: Tina unbuttons Blaine’s shirt and applies vapor rub while he’s asleep
Explanation: She gratifies her crush and maybe helps his cold but violates boundaries and appears creepy.

Event: Tina’s relationship with Mike begins as her eating the eye candy
Explanation: Physical attraction initiates a mutually beneficial romantic relationship.

Event: Tina adopts an Elegant Gothic Lolita aesthetic initially
Explanation: Her style expresses identity and makes her visually distinctive with no shown harm.

Event: Tina drops the Elegant Gothic Lolita look in season 3
Explanation: Updating her look reflects growth and likely improves her social fit without hurting others.

Event: Tina is established as “The Heart” of the group
Explanation: Recognizing and embracing this role boosts her importance and supports group cohesion.

Event: Tina initially rejects being “The Heart”
Explanation: Her reluctance undermines her own potential role slightly while the group still benefits from her nature.

Event: Tina later accepts being “The Heart”
Explanation: Accepting the role strengthens her self-worth and positively influences team morale.

Event: Tina tells Marley “It’s all your fault!” after Sectionals loss
Explanation: She vents her frustration but deeply hurts a vulnerable teammate already blaming herself.

Event: Tina fakes a stutter to avoid oral presentations
Explanation: She dodges stressful tasks but undermines trust and burdens others with her avoidance.

Event: Tina reveals to Artie she’s been faking her stutter and wants to stop
Explanation: Honesty and change improve her integrity and relationships despite short-term conflict.

Event: Artie takes Tina’s fake-stutter revelation badly
Explanation: He protects his feelings by reacting harshly, hurting her and their relationship without real gain.

Event: Tina and Mike become a “Token Minority Couple” that blossoms at Asian camp
Explanation: Their relationship enriches both of them and broadens representation, with no harm shown.

Event: Mike suggests Asian couples therapy as a joke about their token status
Explanation: Lampshading their tokenization keeps things light and self-aware without real damage.

Event: Tina takes a level in Jerkass between junior and senior year
Explanation: She becomes shallower and meaner, gaining social edge at the expense of others’ feelings.

Event: Artie tells Tina that everyone thinks an alien made her shallow and desperate
Explanation: He confronts her behavior with a harsh metaphor that may help awareness but clearly hurts her.

Event: Tina drops Artie for Mike at the start of season 2
Explanation: She improves her own romantic satisfaction while emotionally hurting Artie.

Event: Bree dumps red goo on Tina at prom
Explanation: Bree asserts petty dominance for minimal gain while causing public humiliation for Tina.

Event: Bree targets Tina because another Glee member became Prom Queen
Explanation: Motivated by resentment, she punishes the wrong person and fosters hostility without real benefit.

Event: Tina cries frequently and breaks down at slight provocation
Explanation: Her over-sensitivity hurts her functioning while only marginally eliciting sympathy or support.

Event: Writers make Tina a satellite character with few stories
Explanation: Focusing on others may benefit ratings while limiting her development and presence.

Event: Tina begins to actively rebel against being a satellite character
Explanation: Pushing for more agency helps her and can enrich the narrative for viewers.

Event: Tina starts extremely shy and avoids people with her stutter
Explanation: Her avoidance protects her from anxiety but limits her growth and genuine connections.

Event: Tina gradually grows out of her shyness
Explanation: Overcoming shyness benefits her confidence and her relationships.

Event: Tina chooses Artie as a good-natured partner initially
Explanation: Pursuing a kind partner supports a healthy relationship for both.

Event: People keep making jabs about Tina using vapor rub on Blaine
Explanation: They gain humor/social bonding by repeatedly embarrassing her over a past mistake.

Event: Tina is portrayed as a perky goth and even lampshades it herself
Explanation: Owning and joking about her persona strengthens her identity and social rapport.

Event: Show averts One-Steve Limit by having two unrelated Changs
Explanation: The naming gag adds humor and clarity with no evident harm.

Event: Star Wars-themed opening clarifies Mike and Tina “no relation”
Explanation: Lampshading their shared surname entertains and prevents confusion.

Event: Tina maintains an elaborate, varied wardrobe throughout
Explanation: Her outfits express individuality and add visual interest, helping her image without harm.
